prompt: Write a SELECT query to find all pairs of customers who live in the same city. Return city, name1, a
expected: [{"city":"Chicago","name1":"Carol White","name2":"Frank Wilson"},{"city":"Los Angeles","name1":"Bob Smith","name2":"Eve Davis"},{"city":"Los Angeles",
baseSql: SELECT c1.city, c1.name AS name1, c2.name AS name2 FROM customers c1 JOIN customers c2 ON c1.city = c2.city AND c1.id < c2.id ORDER BY c1.city, name1;
tunedSql: SELECT c1.city, c1.name AS name1, c2.name AS name2 FROM customers c1 JOIN customers c2 ON c1.city = c2.city AND c1.id < c2.id ORDER BY c1.city, name1;

prompt: Write a SELECT query to find the most expensive book. Return title and price.
baseSql: SELECT title, price FROM books ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 1;
tunedSql: SELECT title, price FROM books ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 1;

prompt: Write a SELECT query using IN to find students who are enrolled in courses taught by Dr. Adams. Retu
baseSql: SELECT s.name FROM students s INNER JOIN enrollments e ON s.id = e.student_id INNER JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.id INNER JOIN professors p ON c.professor_
tunedSql: SELECT s.name FROM students s INNER JOIN enrollments e ON s.id = e.student_id INNER JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id INNER JOIN professors p ON p.id = c.pro

prompt: Write a SELECT query using LAG() to show each customer's orders with the date of their previous orde
baseSql: SELECT c.name AS customer_name, o.order_date, LAG(o.order_date) OVER (PARTITION BY c.id ORDER BY o.order_date) AS prev_order_date FROM customers c JOIN orders o
tunedSql: SELECT c.name, o.order_date, LAG(o.order_date) OVER (ORDER BY o.order_date) AS prev_order_date FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id ORDER BY c

prompt: Write a SELECT query to find the average salary per department. Return department and avg_salary.
baseSql: SELECT department, AVG(salary) as avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department;
tunedSql: SELECT department, AVG(salary) as avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department;

prompt: Write a SELECT query to list each book with its price and how much it differs from the average book
expected: [{"title":"The Lord of the Rings","price":20,"price_diff":8},{"title":"One Hundred Years of Solitude","price":15,"price_diff":3},{"title":"Kafka on th
baseSql: WITH book_avg AS ( SELECT AVG(price) AS avg_price FROM books ), book_price_diff AS ( SELECT title, price, price - avg_price AS price_diff FROM books, book_avg )
tunedSql: SELECT title, price, ROUND(price - (SELECT AVG(price) FROM books), 2) AS price_diff FROM books ORDER BY price_diff DESC;

prompt: Write a SELECT query to count how many orders each customer has placed. Return customer_name and ord
baseSql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.id) as order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC;
tunedSql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.id) as order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC;

prompt: Write a SELECT query to find all employees in the Engineering department. Return name and salary.
baseSql: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE department = 'Engineering';
tunedSql: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE department = 'Engineering';

prompt: Write a SELECT query using CROSS JOIN to generate all possible size and color combinations for a pro
baseSql: SELECT s.label AS size_label, c.name AS color_name FROM sizes s CROSS JOIN colors c ORDER BY s.label, c.name;
tunedSql: SELECT s.label, c.name FROM sizes s CROSS JOIN colors c ORDER BY s.label, c.name;
